name: selectolax (Fast HTML/CSS Parser with CSS Selectors)

performance.notes: Lexbor/Modest C backends; 3-10x faster than BeautifulSoup for parsing and querying

agent_readiness.notes: Fast HTML parsing with CSS selectors via C backends. from selectolax.parser import HTMLParser. tree = HTMLParser(html). tree.css('div.main h2'). [node.text() for node in tree.css('p')]. node = tree.css_first('title'). node.text(). node.attributes. node.html. Two backends: Modest (default) and Lexbor (newer, better HTML5). from selectolax.lexbor import LexborHTMLParser. Parses malformed HTML. Much faster than BeautifulSoup for high-throughput web scraping.

gotchas: Less feature-rich than BeautifulSoup — no XPath support
CSS selector support is limited compared to full CSS spec
Two backends (Modest/Lexbor) with slightly different behavior
best_when: High-throughput web scraping where BeautifulSoup is a bottleneck
Large-scale HTML parsing pipelines needing 3-10x speedup
Simple CSS selector extraction from HTML
When parsing speed is the primary concern
avoid_when: Complex tree modification (use BeautifulSoup or lxml)
When BeautifulSoup's extensive documentation and features are needed
summary: selectolax is a fast HTML parser with CSS selectors via Lexbor/Modest C backends — 3-10x faster than BeautifulSoup. HTMLParser(html).css('div.main h2'). css_first('title').text(). Two backends: Modest (stable) and Lexbor (HTML5). MIT. Best for high-throughput scraping where BeautifulSoup is the bottleneck. No XPath support — use lxml for that.
